headlineSummary: HN sentiment skewed negative overall, with Anthropic drawing the strongest criticism over Claude Code and model quality, OpenAI also facing harsh safety and policy criticism despite one positive remark, and Microsoft Copilot showing limited evidence of a weaker negative spillover [E1][E4][E6][E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic is the most negative target here, driven by strong complaints that Claude Code and Opus 4.6 have become unusable or much worse for complex engineering work, plus concerns about transparency [E1][E2][E3].
OpenAI trends negative, with strong criticism about Sora, safety, and alleged hiring decisions, partially offset by one comment praising ChatGPT voice mode [E4][E5][E6].
Microsoft Copilot has limited evidence and reads slightly negative, mainly from one comment that said the same degraded coding behavior was seen "through Copilot" [E3].

evidence.excerpt: Issue: Claude Code is unusable for complex engineering tasks with Feb updates

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names Claude Code and says it is 'unusable' for complex engineering tasks, which is strong criticism of Anthropic's coding product quality.

evidence.excerpt: if Anthropic's subscriptions have dramatically worse behavior than other access to the same model they need to be clear about that. Today there is zero indication from Anthropic that the limitation exists, the redaction was a deliberate feature intended to hide it from the impacted customers

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly accuses Anthropic of hiding subscription limitations and failing to disclose worse behavior, which is strong negative sentiment about trustworthiness and transparency.

evidence.excerpt: Not claude code specific, but I've been noticing this on Opus 4.6 models through Copilot and others as well. Whenever the phrase "simplest fix" appears, it's time to pull the emergency break. This has gotten much, much worse over the past few weeks. It will produce completely useless code, knowingly (because up to that phrase the reasoning was correct) breaking things.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Opus 4.6 and describes it as having gotten 'much, much worse' and producing 'completely useless code,' which is strong criticism of Anthropic model quality.

The commenter says they are seeing the same degraded behavior 'through Copilot,' which directly but secondarily reflects negatively on Microsoft Copilot as the access path experiencing this poor coding output.

evidence.excerpt: For me, the attempted productization of Sora was conclusive proof that 1) OAI was overcapitalized and desperate for revenue 2) safety didn't matter to them much 3) improving the world didn't matter much either.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt ties Sora and 'OAI' to a harsh judgment that safety and mission did not matter, which is strong negative sentiment toward OpenAI.

evidence.excerpt: Altman is absolutely terrified of Black people — not in any contextual sense, but in a visceral, instinctive way. For someone who, as you put it, "controls our future," this should matter. FYI: I am by far not the only one to have experienced this and it 100% impacts hiring and other decisions at OpenAI.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says this alleged behavior affects 'hiring and other decisions at OpenAI,' making it strong negative evidence about OpenAI's employment-related practices.

evidence.excerpt: I do use chatGPT voice mode and it works great for querying any information or discussions.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly praises ChatGPT voice mode as 'works great,' which is mild positive sentiment toward OpenAI's product quality.
